title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are hiring Shane Bowen, former Giants and Titans defensive coordinator, as an analyst.
Bowen previously worked with Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel at the Titans from 2018 to 2020.
Patriots' reshaped defensive staff includes Zak Kuhr as defensive coordinator and Terrell Williams as assistant head coach.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's Age Strong Commission offers an interactive map of public restrooms by neighborhood for convenience and public health.
Individuals with medical conditions can access employee-only restrooms in some Boston retail areas under Massachusetts law.
Restrooms are available in unexpected places, including firehouses and restaurants, emphasizing public restrooms as essential infrastructure.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins alleges Masconomet Regional High School failed to address harassment and threats of sexual violence from peers.
Teachers and educators voiced concerns about the administration's response to gender discrimination and bullying.
Superintendent Michael Harvey disputes claims of inaction, citing privacy regulations as a barrier to comment further.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 billion extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers have paid over $1 billion extra for gasoline since the Iran War began in February.
Gasoline costs in Massachusetts increased from under $2 to over $4 per gallon, significantly impacting household expenses.
The average New England family spent $200 more on home heating oil due to the conflict-related price increase.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massachusetts campgrounds have availability for World Cup fans as hotel prices rise in Boston.
Canoe River Campground in Mansfield, 7 miles from Boston Stadium, offers sites during World Cup matches.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres in Carver provides available glamping cabins and RV sites for soccer fans.

title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty, a stroke survivor, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Rafferty suffered a hemorrhagic stroke in 2019, leaving his left side paralyzed, but recovered with Spaulding's help.
He previously ran the Boston Marathon in 2021, two years after his stroke and credits Spaulding for his recovery.

title: ‘I appreciated that’: Jayson Tatum credited Knicks fans’ applause in MSG return
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden, overcoming pregame nerves, as the Celtics lost 112-106 to the Knicks.
Tatum appreciated the applause from Knicks fans during introductions, a show of respect after his previous injury at MSG.
Tatum reflected on the significance of playing at MSG, linking it to both challenging and meaningful moments in his career.

title: Everett woman arrested after dog attack injures pet and owner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 49-year-old Everett woman was arrested after her dog allegedly attacked another dog and its owner.
The woman's dog, an 11-month-old hound, will be quarantined following the attack as per Massachusetts law.
The injured dog, Mushroom, required 50 stitches and multiple surgeries but is expected to survive.

title: Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, from Maine, drowned while rescuing his children from a riptide in Juno Beach, Florida on April 1.
Emily Jennings shared their love story and his recovery journey, highlighting his transition to working in addiction recovery.
Two GoFundMe campaigns for the Jennings family have raised over $242,000, supporting Emily and their children.

title: A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Outpost Double chairlift at Vermont's Pico Mountain is retired after nearly 60 years due to mechanical failures.
A main drive shaft failure and feasibility issues led to the chairlift's retirement; replacement isn't expected next season.
Pico Mountain plans to auction the Outpost chairs as officials explore temporary solutions to serve the affected terrain.
